output_text:
Subjective:
A 58-year-old female presents with a 3-week history of worsening exertional dyspnea and intermittent palpitations. She denies chest pain, syncope, orthopnea, or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea. Medical history includes hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and hyperlipidemia.

Objective:
Vitals include BP 148/92, HR 102 (irregular), and BMI 31. Physical exam shows an irregularly irregular cardiac rhythm and trace bilateral pretibial pitting edema; lungs are clear. ECG shows atrial fibrillation with a ventricular rate of 98 and no acute ischemic changes. BNP is 312 pg/mL.

Assessment:
58-year
